chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Hello guys, my name is Nitish and you are welcome to my YouTube channel. So, we are continuing with our deep learning course. In the last video, we learned what is the problem with perceptron. And we found that perceptron cannot create non-linear decision boundaries because it is a linear model. It draws a line and does its work.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, what is the solution? The solution is multi-layer perceptron. It means you can connect multiple perceptrons and make a big neural network. And that neural network will work like a universal function approximator and will be able to create any non-linear decision boundary. So, today's video is the first video of this series in the truest sense.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Where you will meet the neural network for the first time. And you will learn why it is able to create non-linear decision boundaries. So, in today's video, I will give you an intuition as to how multi-layer perceptron works. And I will give you an intuition from the very first principle. That is, we will create logic from scratch.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And I have made a lot of diagrams. With the help of those diagrams, I will try to make you understand the whole concept very easily. Also, at the end, I will show you a very good demo on the tensorflow playground. Where you will enjoy watching that demo even more. So, that's it. Let's start the video.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, guys, let's go back to our problem. Our problem is that we have a perceptron as an algorithm. And it also works. But it has only one problem. If that non-linearity is in your data, then it is not able to capture it.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: For example, this is an example on your screen. Here is a dataset where there are two classes. Green and red. But you can see that to separate these two classes, you will need a decision boundary like this. Which is actually not a line.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Which the perceptron draws. In fact, this is a curve. So, our problem is that we have to somehow make such an algorithm. Which can capture any kind of non-linearity. Which our perceptron cannot do.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And the most challenging thing is that we have to make that algorithm using only perceptrons. So, we will try to make such an algorithm using multiple perceptrons. Which can capture any kind of non-linearity. Now, before moving towards multilayer perceptron, I will clear one thing here. In this whole video, the perceptron which I will explain to you.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: The activation function in that perceptron is not a step function. It is a sigmoid. And the loss function is not a hinge loss but a log loss. Basically, I am using my perceptron like a logistic regression. This clarity is very important.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, now let's see how the perceptron works with the sigmoid. Funda is very simple. Let's take the same example again. I have CGPA and IQ. I have to predict whether there will be a placement or not.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Now, how does the sigmoid work? Your output will not be 0 or 1. Or it will not be minus 1 or 1. You will get a probability between 0 and 1. Which will tell whether that child will have a placement or not.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: For example, let me give you an example. Assume that when you trained this model, you got W1 as 5. W2 as 10 and Bias as 3. This is an example. Now, we have this line.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Its equation is 5x plus 10y plus 3 equal to 0. This is the equation of this line. Now, as soon as you give the CGPA of a new child. Assume that CGPA is 8.7 and IQ is 87. So, what will happen?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: First of all, you will calculate Z. How will Z be calculated? Z will be calculated by this formula. W1 multiplied by CGPA. W2 multiplied by IQ and Bias.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: W1 is 5. CGPA is 8.7. W2 is 10. IQ is 87 plus 3. This is Z.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Now, you will send Z to this sigmoid function. This is the formula of sigmoid function. It will give you a probability between 0 and 1. This probability is the probability of placement. Assume that this probability is 0.8.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: We will say that the probability of this student being placed is 0.8. Automatically, since only two things are possible. The second possibility is that its probability of not being placed is 0.2. You just have to understand that for every student, we will get two probabilities. The first probability will be the probability of yes.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: The second probability will be the probability of no. One more thing to understand is that every point here is a student. This line divides this space into two regions. One is this and the other is this. The probability of Y on this line is 0.5.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: If there is a student on this line, then its probability of being placed and not being placed is 0.5. But as you move away from this line to the green region, the probability of being placed is more than the probability of not being placed. If you talk about this student, then its probability of being placed can be 0.9. Automatically, its probability of not being placed will be 0.1.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: But if you talk about this student, since it is close to the line, then its probability of being placed will be 0.6 and its probability of not being placed will be 0.4. Similarly, if you talk about this student, since it is very far from the line in this direction, then its probability of being placed will be very less and its probability of not being placed will be more. If you talk about this student, then its probability of being placed will be 0.4

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: and its probability of not being placed will be 0.6. So, this becomes a sort of gradient. The more you move away from the line, the more your probability increases. For example, if you look at all these lines, then the probability of being placed will be 0.6 because this has 0.5, this has 0.7, this has 0.8, and this has 0.9.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Similarly, if you draw parallel lines here, then it will be 0.4 here. If you draw this line, then it will be 0.5 here. If you draw this line, then it will be 0.3 here, 0.2 here, and 0.1 here. So, that is how Perceptron works. So, you just have to understand that for every student, you will get two probabilities.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: The first probability is your probability of yes, and by subtracting 1 from it, you can get the probability of no. Okay, I had to explain this to you. I hope you understood this. So guys, we have understood how Perceptron works with Sigmoid. Now, what do we do? We try to form an idea of Multilayer Perceptron. So, let's say I have this data, which I showed you a while ago.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And let's say I have trained two different Perceptrons on this data. This is my first Perceptron, and this is my second Perceptron. Assume for a while that here W1 is 2, W2 is 3, and Bias is 6 in this model. And in this model, W1 is let's say 5, W2 is let's say 4, and B is 3. So, the line of this Perceptron looks like this.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: This is the decision boundary of this Perceptron. So, the equation of this line will be 2x plus 3y plus 6. Similarly, this is the decision boundary of the other Perceptron. I got this line, so we can write its equation as 5x plus 4y plus 3 equal to 0. Now, what I am saying is, what if we somehow connect the output of these two Perceptrons, the decision boundary,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: and by doing so, something like this happens. Now, look at this and you will understand. What I have done is that I have taken this image below and superimposed it on top of this. And I am getting this kind of new decision boundary. Now, what I will do is, I will do a smoothening in this.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Now, what is smoothening, you will understand a little later. And as soon as I do smoothening, I will see this output, this one. And guess what, I wanted this decision boundary. So, whatever I have discussed with you, this is the abstract idea of multilayer Perceptrons. Now, whatever we have discussed, there is no mathematical logic behind it.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: I mean, I have not explained to you how this superimposition is happening or how this smoothening is happening. I have not told you all this. I have not given you its technical details, mathematical details. But I just want to give you an idea, I want to give you an intuition as to what is happening. So, what we are doing is, to solve the same problem, we are taking more than one Perceptron. In our case, we are taking two Perceptrons.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: The decision boundaries of both are different, as you can see. And now, what we are doing is, first of all, we are superimposing them. We are placing them on top of each other. And then, what we are doing is, we are doing smoothening. Because of which, I am getting my desired output.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: But whatever we have discussed, this is in the air. There is no mathematical logic behind it, I have not justified it yet. So, now I will show you how this can be achieved mathematically. So, to prove the mathematics behind this, I have placed these two Perceptrons in front of you. Now, let's do one thing.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: All the points here are basically our students. So, let's say we have caught this student. Now, according to this particular Perceptron, the probability of placement of this student, let's say, is 0.7. Similarly, according to this Perceptron of this student, the probability of placement is 0.8. Similarly, for every student, there will be a probability of placement.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Now, what I have done is, I have caught this student again and added the output of these two. So, what I have done is, if I have to find out the probability of placement of this student in my new model, then I will add the output of this Perceptron to the output of this Perceptron and this will be my probability. There is only one problem that here it is 1.5 and you know that the probability is always between 0 and 1. So, this is a problem.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: But we also have a solution for this. What if I take this probability i and put it in the sigmoid function. The formula for the sigmoid function is 1 plus 1 divided by 1 plus e to the power minus z. So, in this case, if you want to find out, since I have calculated it, it would come out to be 0.82. So, now the combined model that we have made, where I first added these two probabilities and then put it in the sigmoid,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: so according to that new model, the probability of the placement of this student is coming out to be 0.82. And this is what I will do with every student. Now I will take this student, I will find out its probability of yes given by Perceptron 1. Then I will find out its probability with Perceptron 2. I will add both of them and put them in the sigmoid and I will get its probability of placement.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, that is how you achieve this model. So, here you did two things, first you did addition and then put it back in the sigmoid, because of which the two things behind are happening, superimposition and smoothening. So, I hope you are getting a little clear here that how this whole thing is working mathematically. You are basically finding out the linear combination.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: You are using the linear combination of two perceptrons to find out the new probability. And this is its resultant. So guys, now the concept that we discussed, the linear combination of two perceptrons, let's extend it a little more. I want to add a little flexibility to it.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Let's say I want this decision boundary instead of my decision boundary. So, if you look carefully, this decision boundary is such that the impact of Perceptron 1 is more on it and the impact of Perceptron 2 is less. So, what I mean to say is that can I change something in this model so that the output of Perceptron 1 dominates more than the output of Perceptron 2.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: The answer is you can definitely do it. What can you do? What were we doing till now? We were picking up any student and taking the probability of yes from both the places. We were adding it and then sending it to sigmoid and that was our probability. What if we make this addition a weighted addition?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: That means we don't directly send this probability here. We send it through a weight. Let's say I gave this weight of 10 and gave this weight of 5. So, now what I am saying is that my new model, the multi-layer Perceptron model that is being made, the weightage of Perceptron 1 is twice that of Perceptron 2.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, now the new combination that will be made here will look like this. We will take the output of Perceptron 1 and multiply it by 10 because this is its weight. Plus, we will take the output of Perceptron 2 and multiply it by 5. And this number that will come, we are calling it z. Now, we will send it to sigmoid.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And then the probability that will come will be my probability of yes. And I will do this with every point. In fact, what you can do is that you can add bias to this. That means you can say that when the output comes from here, the weight will be 10. When the output comes from here, it will be 5.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And there is a bias in this system whose value is let's say 3. So, now what will happen? Its value is 0.7. Its value is 0.8. So, now this whole expression will become z here.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: 0.7 times 10 plus 0.8 times 5 plus this bias. And this bias came here. This is my z. I sent it to sigmoid. And then it will give me a probability from 0 to 1 for this student.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, for every student here, I will repeat the same thing. Now, if you look carefully. So, what is happening is that this whole thing is itself working as a perceptron. If you think about it. This whole thing, here you are getting the first input.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Here you are getting the second input. This is your W1. This is your W2. And here is a B. And there is a sigmoid in front of it.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And then you are taking out the output. So, in a way, what are you doing? From these two perceptrons, the output coming from this perceptron and this perceptron. You are providing it as input in this new perceptron. So, in a way, what is happening is that you are making a combination of three perceptrons.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Which we can call a multi-layer perceptron. In fact, I do one thing. I draw this whole thing. So, I am getting x1 from here. I am getting x2 from here.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Here is my W1. Sorry, this is my W1. This is my W2. Its value is 5. Its value is 10.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: This is my summation here. Where the value of the bias is 3. And from here, we are taking out the sigmoid. This is my z. And this is my y hat.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: This is my output. Now, there is only one thing here. This input is not getting from the data. It is getting from perceptron 1. And this is getting from perceptron 2.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, in a way, you can make a mental picture like this. That perceptron 1 and perceptron 2 is connected to perceptron 3. And over here, you are getting your output. So, now this is actually a multi-layer perceptron. Now, guys, I will draw the whole picture clearly in front of you.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, this is my perceptron 1. This is my perceptron 2. This is my new result from the linear combination of these two. So, if you want to draw this through nodes, weights, and biases, then how will it look?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: First of all, I will show you this perceptron. So, we have the same data. It is CGPA. It is IQ. And in the output, we have the placement or not.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: This is the data. So, this perceptron will look something like this. Here, one input will be of CGPA. The other input will be of IQ. By giving bias here rather than 1,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: what I will do is that I will keep writing bias inside. By the way, bias in this case will be 6. This weight will be 2. And this weight will be 3. Okay?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And from here, I am getting the output of this perceptron. This perceptron. Okay? Basically, it is a probability because there is a sigmoid. Similarly, if you talk about this perceptron,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: then it will look like this. Its bias is 3. It will also get the input of CGPA. And it will also get the input of IQ. And it will also give me the output.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Okay? Now, if you talk about this, this is also a perceptron. The bias of this perceptron is 3. And it will get input from two places.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Basically, one from the output of this perceptron and one from its output. Okay? And it will also give me the output here. Okay?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Also, this weight is 10. And this weight is 5. Now, what we can do is we can redraw this whole thing and write it like this. So, it would be 6, 3.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: We have drawn these two connections. 3. Output. CGPA. IQ.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: CGPA. IQ. Okay? In fact, we can draw this better here. Bias is 6.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Bias is 3. Bias is 3. This is the connection. Here, weight is 10. Here, weight is 5.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Here, you will get the output. Now, CGPA is also getting this. CGPA is also getting this. So, what we will do is we will make this CGPA.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And we will make this IQ. And the input of CGPA is going to both places. Similarly, the output is also going to both places. Also, the weights are defined here as well. CGPA to first is 2.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And IQ to first is 3. CGPA to second is 5. So, this one will be 5. And this one will be 4. Okay?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And guys, you can see this is what we call a multi-layer perceptron. Because there are multiple layers here. This is what we call an input layer. This is the input layer.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: This is what we call an output layer. And this is our hidden layer. Okay? And that's it. This is what you have to understand.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: How you can organize more than one perceptron in this way and capture non-linearity. Okay? So, I tried to give you an intuition

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: with a kind of first principle approach that how a multi-layer perceptron can capture non-linearity. The concept is very simple. You make linear combinations

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: of multiple perceptrons. Okay? I hope this idea is clear to you. Okay? Now, what I will do is

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: I will make this structure a little more complex and show you what you can achieve next. Okay? Which will be very, very interesting. Guys, now we will study

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: how we can bring changes in the architecture of our neural network to have some additional flexibility. First of all, it is important for you to understand what is the architecture of the neural network.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, the basic meaning of architecture is that your nodes, which you also call perceptrons, how they are connected to each other. Okay?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: The connection between weights and nodes is called architecture. So, what you can do is you can bring changes in your architecture based on your requirement.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, I will tell you four ways how you can bring changes. So, the first way is that the hidden layer you have, this hidden layer,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: you can increase the number of nodes in it. Okay? Increasing nodes means increasing the number of perceptrons. Like this example,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: which is on your screen. All the discussions we have done so far, there were a total of two perceptrons in our hidden layer. But in this case, as you can see,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: there are three perceptrons. Everything else is the same. The data is the same. What I did this time is I added an extra perceptron in the hidden layer.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Now, what is happening because of that is if I have such data which is very non-linear, then I am able to capture the additional non-linearity.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, this is the decision boundary of my first perceptron, this is the decision boundary of the second, and this is the decision boundary of the third. When I created a linear combination of these three,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: then I got this output. Now, this is not different. It is exactly the same thing. Like, if you capture this point, then the probability of this point being green

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: by this perceptron could be, let's say, 0.2. The probability of this being green would be, let's say, 0.3. This could be 0.4.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Here, the weights are given, let's say, 1, 2, 3. So, what did we do again? We calculated Z for the perceptron here. And how did we calculate Z?

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: 0.2 multiplied by 1, 0.3 multiplied by 2, 0.4 multiplied by 3, plus its own bias, let's say, bias is 10.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, this is how we calculated Z. Now, to calculate Z, this extra term was used because there was an extra perceptron. Now, I sent Z to the sigmoid

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: and the sigmoid, for this point, gave a probability between 0 and 1. Everything is exactly the same, except for this extra term. If we add another node tomorrow,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: then we will have to add another term here while calculating Z. So, the thing to understand is that first, you can add nodes in your hidden layer.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: You can add as many as you want. And the second thing to understand is that the more nodes you add, the more help you will get in making more complex non-linear decision boundaries

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: as I have shown you in this example. The second way to change the architecture of your neural network is to increase the number of nodes in your input layer.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: But you will only do this when the input columns in your data increase. Like, till now, we were talking that we have CGPA as input column and IQ as input column.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: If we had another input column in our data, which is 12K marks, then in our input column, sorry, in our input layer, we will have 3 nodes.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And now, all this data will go in 3D. So, let's say, on the x-axis, CGPA, on the y-axis, IQ, and on the z-axis,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: your 12K marks. Okay? And I told you that if it is in 3D, then your perceptron model will be a plane.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And you can see that this is a plane. Similarly, this is another perceptron model. This is also a plane. Now, what I will do is, I will combine these two planes linearly.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Again, I will get a linear combination. So, if you take any point, then for the probability of that point being green or red, first of all, I will get

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: its probability. I will multiply it by W1 and I will do the same thing with W2, its probability. Again, I will calculate Z.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Again, I will calculate Sigma of Z. And for that, I will get a probability between 0 and 1. Okay? So, here, it is a very simple thing that the more

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: columns there are in your input, the more nodes there will be in the input layer. Okay? And if there was one more, then it would have become 4D. And then it would have become a hyperplane instead of a plane.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Okay? Obviously, we cannot visualize it. But we can see it up to 3D. Okay? So, this is the second case where you are increasing the number of nodes in the input layer. The third way is to change

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: the architecture of the neural network that you increase the number of nodes in your output layer. Okay? So, till now, in all the examples that we have discussed,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: we have always noticed that in the last one, there is only one perceptron. But it is not necessary. If you want, you can keep more than one perceptron. Usually, you do this when you are doing

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: multi-class classification. For example, you have to look at a photo and tell whether there is a dog in that photo or a cat or a human.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, what you will do is you will make a perceptron attached to all three. This is for a dog, this is for a cat and this is for a human.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: In the end, whichever of these has a higher probability, for that particular photo, you will say that it is a dog or a cat or a human.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, what you have to learn is that in the case of multi-class classification, you keep multiple nodes in your output layer. Okay? The fourth way is to

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: change the architecture of the neural network that you increase the number of hidden layers. Till now, we have not increased a single layer. We were increasing

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: nodes in the layer. But if you want, you can increase the number of hidden layers. Here, you see this is the input layer and this is the output layer. But here, I have

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: two hidden layers. This is hidden layer 1 and this is my hidden layer 2. Okay? What will be the

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: benefit of doing this? The benefit will be that when you have very complex non-linear data which you can get out of a very complex decision boundary,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: in that case, you can create such a complex decision boundary only by adding layers. So, you can see that your first layer, hidden layer,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: is only creating linear decision boundaries. But as you go deep in your neural network, you will be able to capture complex relationships.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: And then, if they have a linear combination, then more complex relationships can be captured. So, for this reason, neural networks are called

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: universal function approximators. You can give any complex data which has a lot of non-linearity. If you add enough

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: hidden layers in your network, then given enough time, if you give enough time in training, then your neural network will capture that relationship.

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: Okay? So, this is a very important concept that you can create a deep neural network. Obviously, there are considerations that training time will be more

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: and a lot of other things will come in the picture. But this is always true that given enough hidden layers and enough training time,

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: you can create any mathematical function with your neural network. with your neural network. Guys, to show you this demo, I have again brought you to

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: the Tensorflow playground. So, if you remember, I had shown you a demo in Perceptron that how a single

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition
chunks.text: So, now I will show you that even a small multilayer Perceptron can easily handle this data. Okay? So, here you can see

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition

chunks.title: Multi Layer Perceptron | MLP Intuition

text: Hello guys, my name is Nitish and you are welcome to my YouTube channel. So, we are continuing with our deep learning course. In the last video, we learned what is the problem with perceptron. And we found that perceptron cannot create non-linear decision boundaries because it is a linear model. It draws a line and does its work. So, what is the solution? The solution is multi-layer perceptron. It means you can connect multiple perceptrons and make a big neural network. And that neural network will work like a universal function approximator and will be able to create any non-linear decision boundary. So, today's video is the first video of this series in the truest sense. Where you will meet the neural network for the first time. And you will learn why it is able to create non-linear decision boundaries. So, in today's video, I will give you an intuition as to how multi-layer perceptron works. And I will give you an intuition from the very first principle. That is, we will create logic from scratch. And I have made a lot of diagrams. With the help of those diagrams, I will try to make you understand the whole concept very easily. Also, at the end, I will show you a very good demo on the tensorflow playground. Where you will enjoy watching that demo even more. So, that's it. Let's start the video. So, guys, let's go back to our problem. Our problem is that we have a perceptron as an algorithm. And it also works. But it has only one problem. If that non-linearity is in your data, then it is not able to capture it. For example, this is an example on your screen. Here is a dataset where there are two classes. Green and red. But you can see that to separate these two classes, you will need a decision boundary like this. Which is actually not a line. Which the perceptron draws. In fact, this is a curve. So, our problem is that we have to somehow make such an algorithm. Which can capture any kind of non-linearity. Which our perceptron cannot do. And the most challenging thing is that we have to make that algorithm using only perceptrons. So, we will try to make such an algorithm using multiple perceptrons. Which can capture any kind of non-linearity. Now, before moving towards multilayer perceptron, I will clear one thing here. In this whole video, the perceptron which I will explain to you. The activation function in that perceptron is not a step function. It is a sigmoid. And the loss function is not a hinge loss but a log loss. Basically, I am using my perceptron like a logistic regression. This clarity is very important. So, now let's see how the perceptron works with the sigmoid. Funda is very simple. Let's take the same example again. I have CGPA and IQ. I have to predict whether there will be a placement or not. Now, how does the sigmoid work? Your output will not be 0 or 1. Or it will not be minus 1 or 1. You will get a probability between 0 and 1. Which will tell whether that child will have a placement or not. For example, let me give you an example. Assume that when you trained this model, you got W1 as 5. W2 as 10 and Bias as 3. This is an example. Now, we have this line. Its equation is 5x plus 10y plus 3 equal to 0. This is the equation of this line. Now, as soon as you give the CGPA of a new child. Assume that CGPA is 8.7 and IQ is 87. So, what will happen? First of all, you will calculate Z. How will Z be calculated? Z will be calculated by this formula. W1 multiplied by CGPA. W2 multiplied by IQ and Bias. W1 is 5. CGPA is 8.7. W2 is 10. IQ is 87 plus 3. This is Z. Now, you will send Z to this sigmoid function. This is the formula of sigmoid function. It will give you a probability between 0 and 1. This probability is the probability of placement. Assume that this probability is 0.8. We will say that the probability of this student being placed is 0.8. Automatically, since only two things are possible. The second possibility is that its probability of not being placed is 0.2. You just have to understand that for every student, we will get two probabilities. The first probability will be the probability of yes. The second probability will be the probability of no. One more thing to understand is that every point here is a student. This line divides this space into two regions. One is this and the other is this. The probability of Y on this line is 0.5. If there is a student on this line, then its probability of being placed and not being placed is 0.5. But as you move away from this line to the green region, the probability of being placed is more than the probability of not being placed. If you talk about this student, then its probability of being placed can be 0.9. Automatically, its probability of not being placed will be 0.1. But if you talk about this student, since it is close to the line, then its probability of being placed will be 0.6 and its probability of not being placed will be 0.4. Similarly, if you talk about this student, since it is very far from the line in this direction, then its probability of being placed will be very less and its probability of not being placed will be more. If you talk about this student, then its probability of being placed will be 0.4 and its probability of not being placed will be 0.6. So, this becomes a sort of gradient. The more you move away from the line, the more your probability increases. For example, if you look at all these lines, then the probability of being placed will be 0.6 because this has 0.5, this has 0.7, this has 0.8, and this has 0.9. Similarly, if you draw parallel lines here, then it will be 0.4 here. If you draw this line, then it will be 0.5 here. If you draw this line, then it will be 0.3 here, 0.2 here, and 0.1 here. So, that is how Perceptron works. So, you just have to understand that for every student, you will get two probabilities.
